title: stelis candida
intro: Stelis candida, commonly known as the white orchid, is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in reducing stress and promoting relaxation. The therapeutic actions of Stelis candida include sedative and antispasmodic effects, which may help to alleviate anxiety and muscle spasms. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
benefits: Stelis candida has many benefits, such as its potential to improve respiratory health by relieving symptoms of bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling in the body. Additionally, this plant is believed to have antioxidant and antibacterial properties, which can aid in the prevention of various diseases, including cancer and infections. The benefits of Stelis candida also extend to its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, and its ability to boost the immune system, making it easier to fight off illnesses. By incorporating Stelis candida into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved overall health and well-being, leading to a more energetic and productive life.
parts: Stelis candida has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, roots, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The leaves of the Stelis candida plant are known to contain flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of the plant contain alkaloids, including stelisin and stelidine, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The flowers of Stelis candida contain glycosides, including steloside and stelisin glycoside, which have been used to treat respiratory issues and have been found to have expectorant properties.
preparations: Stelis candida has many herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are utilized for their medicinal properties. The tea is commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Tinctures of the plant are used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, including indigestion and nausea, due to their carminative effects. Additionally, infusions of the plant have been traditionally used to treat fever and reduce inflammation.
side_effects: Stelis candida can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can also cause headaches, dizziness, and confusion, which can be symptoms of neurotoxicity. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic properties may lead to liver damage and other serious health problems. In severe cases, improper use of Stelis candida can result in respiratory failure, seizures, and even death.
